steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between "an ecological" and the verb phrase "compared the lengths of eggs... found Calidris alpina eggs to be longer..." The blank must connect "study" to that verb phrase using the correct relative pronoun and punctuation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — study that: Restrictive relative clauses use that without a comma when the clause identifies which specific study is meant. Here, the clause tells us exactly what the study did — essential identifying information. No comma needed. ✓
  • Choice B — study: Omitting the relative pronoun leaves "an ecological study compared the lengths of eggs produced by different bird species found...", which gives the second clause two finite verbs ("compared" and "found") without coordination or subordination. ✗
  • Choice C — study, which: Non-restrictive clauses with which and a comma signal additional, nonessential information. But this clause identifies what the study did — it is essential, not extra. ✗
  • Choice D — study, that: That never follows a comma in a relative clause. This is ungrammatical in Standard English. ✗
That matches Choice A — study that.
